name: Covered porch
description: An open-air covered porch attached to the front facade, sheltering the main entrance under a roof carried by two to four slender posts rather than full walls. The deck typically sits one to two blocks above ground level, reached by a short run of stairs, so the threshold reads as a raised, dry platform that mediates between yard and interior. Low railings or balustrades may edge the open sides at waist height, but the porch is never enclosed: light, breeze and view pass freely between the posts. The roof slope matches the main building roof so the porch reads as an extension of the silhouette rather than an applique. Strongly associated with American Foursquare, Victorian, and rustic farmhouse vernaculars; provides a gracious, weather-protected pause at the front door.
